claim: Steve Jobs was intolerant of mediocre work not mediocre people - excellent workers found him the best manager, poorly-performing ones were fired
actionable: When building teams, set high quality standards and surround yourself with excellence rather than people-pleasing
source_title: Marc Andreessen on AI, the Future of Startups, and Why Timing Is Everything...

claim: Jobs learned management discipline from 12 years at NeXT before returning to Apple - failure and constraint forced him to master leadership the hard way
actionable: Embrace difficult periods as opportunities to develop fundamental skills rather than coasting on past success
source_title: Marc Andreessen on AI, the Future of Startups, and Why Timing Is Everything...

claim: Market timing is the primary determinant of startup success - early is the same as being wrong, and most overnight successes were preceded by 5+ failed attempts
actionable: Research whether market conditions (technology readiness, user adoption) support your idea timing, not just feasibility
source_title: Marc Andreessen on AI, the Future of Startups, and Why Timing Is Everything...

claim: Smartphones had 20 years of failed attempts (1987-2007) before iPhone succeeded - capability doesn't guarantee timing is right
actionable: Monitor market adoption signals rather than relying on technical capability to gauge if timing is right for a startup
source_title: Marc Andreessen on AI, the Future of Startups, and Why Timing Is Everything...

claim: Founders must balance living in the future (envisioning unrealized possibilities) with being constrained by current market readiness
actionable: Develop entrepreneurial judgment by continuously assessing both your vision and market preconditions
source_title: Marc Andreessen on AI, the Future of Startups, and Why Timing Is Everything...

claim: Good startup founders feel they are racing against time (too late) rather than too early, which is actually a positive signal
actionable: If you feel the market is coming and your product isn't ready, that's actually a sign you're well-timed, not behind
source_title: Marc Andreessen on AI, the Future of Startups, and Why Timing Is Everything...

claim: Platform shifts (AI, crypto, new tech paradigms) create the best opportunities for startups to outcompete incumbents
actionable: As a founder, identify platform shifts happening now and build on top of them rather than optimizing legacy platforms
source_title: Marc Andreessen on AI, the Future of Startups, and Why Timing Is Everything...

claim: Neural networks could have provided intelligence assistance for 30 years (since 1990s) but sociological readiness wasn't there until ChatGPT
actionable: Understand that technical capability doesn't determine market success - cultural and sociological adoption timing is equally critical
source_title: Marc Andreessen on AI, the Future of Startups, and Why Timing Is Everything...

claim: The next 4 years (2025-2029) present unprecedented opportunity for startups due to AI platform shift and supportive policy environment
actionable: Start AI companies now - the convergence of ready technology, market adoption, and favorable policy creates optimal conditions
source_title: Marc Andreessen on AI, the Future of Startups, and Why Timing Is Everything...
